keyPoints: Surface diverse, non-aggregated AI outputs with explicit uncertainty and provenance cues to surface error modes and calibration signals.
Treat trust calibration as alignment between AI reliability and user reliance, prioritizing avoidance of overtrust and undertrust.
Incorporate cognitive forcing functions (e.g., Active Verification) to reduce overreliance and improve decision accuracy.
Plan a 6–12 month longitudinal, cross-domain study across high- and low-stakes domains with multiple waves.
Build epistemic-hygiene training and exposure to diverse perspectives to foster verification habits and misinformation resilience.
Use calibration metrics (Brier score, calibration curves) and resilience measures (misinformation belief revision) alongside task performance.
Ensure governance/ethics through transparency about uncertainty, provenance, and mis/disinformation risk.
